entry.title: Mark Zuckerberg’s new goal is creating artificial general intelligence
entry.quote: “We’ve come to this view that, in order to build the products that we want to build, we need to build for general intelligence, I think that’s important to convey because a lot of the best researchers want to work on the more ambitious problems.”

entry.title: Mourning Google
entry.quote: Larry and Sergey were smart guys who recognized they didn’t know shit about corporateness and quickly got into a pattern of hiring and empowering psychotic pricks who were presumably “good at business”.

entry.title: Unseen images of code breaking computer that helped win WW2
entry.quote: Despite its huge impact, engineers and codebreakers who had worked on the Colossus programme were sworn to secrecy and the existence of this vital piece of machinery was kept from the history books for almost six decades.

entry.title: Researchers confirm what we already knew: Google (product) results really are getting worse
entry.quote: The boffins made their determination after spending a year reviewing results for 7,392 product review queries on Google, Bing and DuckDuckGo, which they said is the first systematic review into the question of worsening search engine result quality.

entry.title: Workers are filming their layoffs, then posting them to TikTok. What could go wrong?
entry.quote: In an era of remote work, some employees are recording and posting their video layoffs and firings on social media. It can be empowering in a lonely situation, but is it wise?
